items.title: Mutation testing for the agentic era
items.summary: Code coverage is one of the most dangerous quality metrics in software testing. Many developers fail to realize that code coverage lies by omission: it measures execution, not verification. Test suites with high coverage can obfuscate the fact that critical functionality is untes

items.title: Try our new dimensional analysis Claude plugin
items.summary: We’re releasing a new Claude plugin for developing and auditing code that implements dimensional analysis, a technique we explored in our most recent blog post. Most LLM-based security skills ask the model to find bugs. Our new dimensional-analysis plugin for Claude Code takes a

items.title: Spotting issues in DeFi with dimensional analysis
items.summary: Using dimensional analysis, you can categorically rule out a whole category of logic and arithmetic bugs that plague DeFi formulas. No code changes required, just better reasoning! One of the first lessons in physics is learning to think in terms of dimensions. Physicists can oft

items.title: Paying Iran in crypto could put shippers at sanctions risk: Chainalysis
items.summary: Crypto’s transparent ledger makes sanctions evasion easier to trace, allowing authorities to track and potentially freeze illicit flows.

items.title: Trump-linked WLFI hits new low as token-backed loan triggers concern
items.summary: WLFI fell to a record low after it was revealed that the project used billions of its own tokens as collateral to borrow $75 million in stablecoins.

items.title: Bittensor's TAO risks 45% dip amid 'decentralization theater' accusation
items.summary: TAO drops 30% from its weekly high, confirming fractal setups that projected deeper downside targets for the token in the past.

items.title: White House warns staff as Iran bets add to growing insider trading concerns
items.summary: Reuters reported that White House staff were warned against using confidential information after suspicious Iran-linked oil futures bets and fresh scrutiny of prediction markets.

items.title: XRP drops to $1.33 as bitcoin weakness pulls down majors
items.summary: Sudden selloff triggered liquidation-style move, with weak recovery reinforcing bearish structure despite compressed volatility setup.

items.title: Bitcoin and other cryptos fall as U.S., Iranian negotiators fail to reach war resolution
items.summary: U.S. Vice President J.D. Vance said negotiations had ended after a daylong session in Pakistan, sending crypto prices down between 1.5% and 2%.

items.title: Bitcoin, Ether near levels that could signal trend reversal: Analyst
items.summary: Bitcoin and Ether are each less than 10% away from price levels that could signal a potential reversal in the crypto market, according to a macro analyst.

items.title: Musk’s SpaceX holds $603 million in bitcoin despite $5 billion loss stemming from xAI
items.summary: Arkham data shows 8,285 BTC in Coinbase Prime custody as the company swings from $8 billion profit to nearly $5 billion loss ahead of its IPO push.

items.title: Bitcoin signals potential seller exhaustion as realized losses decline
items.summary: On-chain data points to easing selling pressure, with realized losses falling and spot markets shifting toward net buying.

items.title: The crypto honeymoon is over for now as analysts warn of a major first-quarter profit squeeze
items.summary: Several major investment firms have preemptively downgraded Coinbase and other platforms as a sharp drop in trading activity and falling token prices threaten to derail upcoming first-quarter earnings results.

items.title: $1.6 billion Ether Machine SPAC deal collapses over unfavorable market
items.summary: The Ether Machine, an ether treasury firm, currently has more than $1 billion in ether in its treasury.

items.title: Bitcoin, broader market flat as U.S.-Iran negotiations begin
items.summary: The market rose over the week after a two-week ceasefire was announced, triggering a derivatives short squeeze that wiped out over $430 million in bearish positions.

items.title: Iran war oil-price shock revives inflation trade and a new stablecoin play
items.summary: As oil shocks revive investor anxiety, stablecoins solved payments, but not purchasing power, says Michael Ashton, who's USDi token aims to fix that.

items.title: Crypto perpetuals predict the direction of Wall Street’s Monday open with 89% accuracy, data shows
items.summary: Over half (57%) of Monday market opens is s already reflected in crypto markets' perpetual futures products.

items.title: Crypto Clarity bill has 30% chance of passing this year, Wintermute’s Hammond says
items.summary: Wintermute’s head of policy Ron Hammond pegs chances at 30%, citing political friction, stalled negotiations and shifting timelines despite signs of progress.

items.title: Bitwise files updated S-1 for Hyperliquid ETF as HYPE fund race heats up
items.summary: HYPE has surged around 200% over the last 12 months. Other asset managers including Grayscale, 21Shares, and VanEck are also eyeing  HYPE-linked ETFs.

items.title: The bitcoin market is splitting in two. Here's who is buying and selling amid the war
items.summary: Six weeks of war have revealed that bitcoin's floor depends entirely on a handful of mandated buyers absorbing what everyone else is trying to get rid of.
